limits(3) Library Functions Manual limits(3)

NAME

limits - Application Limits


- Limits for clients, frame rates, display names, and other constraints.

SYNOPSIS

Files


file limits.h
Application limits and constraints.

Macros


#define MAX_DISPLAY_NAME_LEN 32
Maximum display name length in characters. #define MAX_CLIENT_ID_LEN 64
Maximum client ID length - format: noun.N(transport:port)"e.g.,mountain.0 (tcp:48036). #define MAX_CLIENTS 32
Maximum possible clients (static array size) - actual runtime limit set by --max-clients (1-32). #define DEFAULT_MAX_FPS 60
Default maximum frame rate (frames per second). #define FRAME_INTERVAL_MS (1000 / DEFAULT_MAX_FPS)
Frame interval in milliseconds based on default FPS (compile-time constant). #define FRAME_BUFFER_CAPACITY (DEFAULT_MAX_FPS / 4)
Frame buffer capacity based on default FPS (compile-time constant).

Detailed Description

Limits for clients, frame rates, display names, and other constraints.

Macro Definition Documentation

#define DEFAULT_MAX_FPS 60

#include <include/ascii-chat/common/limits.h>

Default maximum frame rate (frames per second).

Referenced by threaded_send_terminal_size_with_auto_detect().

#define FRAME_BUFFER_CAPACITY (DEFAULT_MAX_FPS / 4)

#include <include/ascii-chat/common/limits.h>

Frame buffer capacity based on default FPS (compile-time constant).

Definition at line 35 of file limits.h.

#define FRAME_INTERVAL_MS (1000 / DEFAULT_MAX_FPS)

#include <include/ascii-chat/common/limits.h>

Frame interval in milliseconds based on default FPS (compile-time constant).

#define MAX_CLIENT_ID_LEN 64

#include <include/ascii-chat/common/limits.h>

Maximum client ID length - format: "noun.N (transport:port)" e.g., "mountain.0 (tcp:48036)".
